THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Marathon County learned it was denied FEMA public assistance after the April storms — though individual aid remains available — while Kronenwetter and Weston boards moved ahead on infrastructure spending and development decisions.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; 24 county residents have already applied at disasterassistance.gov.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board approved an amended bid of $814,815 for a six-bay equipment garage, adding a second restroom, and approved a ballot drop box for the August primary and November general elections.
  • Weston's Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue/Bayberry Street reconstruction to HS Suns for approximately $2,248,819.90, including a sidewalk alternate, and awarded a $12,500 surveying contract for Howland Avenue.
  • Weston's Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090 sq ft detached accessory dwelling unit at 4804 Shore Avenue and forwarded a 23-lot Bull Junior View subdivision plat to the village board.
  • Kronenwetter's Plan Commission is set to consider a zoning change for Marathon County Highway Department property from B3, SF and AR to M2 General Industrial and a conditional use permit for a residential pond.

Coming up: Monday brings full village board meetings in both Weston and Kronenwetter, along with Wausau's Public Health & Safety Committee at 5:15 p.m.; Tuesday, Wausau's Plan Commission meets at 5:00 p.m. and Kronenwetter holds a revised Administrative Policy Committee session at 5:30 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
